summary: Completed e2e testing of file upload feature for B2B Cardamom Export platform. All 11 backend API tests passed (100%). All 10 frontend UI tests passed (100%). File upload for product management is fully functional.
passed_tests: BACKEND: POST /api/upload requires admin authentication (401 without auth)
BACKEND: POST /api/upload accepts PNG image with admin auth, returns {path, content_type, original_filename}
BACKEND: POST /api/upload accepts JPEG image with admin auth
BACKEND: POST /api/upload rejects .txt files (400 status with 'not allowed' message)
BACKEND: POST /api/upload rejects .pdf files (400 status)
BACKEND: GET /api/files/{path} serves uploaded file with correct content type
BACKEND: GET /api/files/{path} returns 404 for nonexistent files
BACKEND: GET /api/products returns products with media_paths array field
BACKEND: POST /api/admin/products creates product with media_paths
BACKEND: PUT /api/admin/products/{id} updates product media_paths
BACKEND: Customer (non-admin) cannot upload files (403 Forbidden)
FRONTEND: Admin Products tab shows 'Add Product' button
FRONTEND: Add Product form has file upload area (not URL input)
FRONTEND: File upload button shows 'Add Photos/Videos' with counter (0/4)
FRONTEND: Form shows 'Min 1, Max 4 — JPG, PNG, WEBP, MP4, MOV' label
FRONTEND: Minimum 1 file validation - shows error toast 'Please add at least 1 image or video'
FRONTEND: Admin can select files and see preview thumbnails before saving
FRONTEND: File preview shows correctly with upload counter updating to (1/4)
FRONTEND: Admin can edit existing product and see existing media previews
FRONTEND: Products page shows product images (backward compatible with image_url)
FRONTEND: Full upload flow works - product created with uploaded file appears in list
seed_data_creation: Test product 'TEST_FullUpload_Product' was created and deleted during testing. Uploaded test files remain in object storage.
context_for_next_testing_agent: File upload feature fully tested and working. Key endpoints: POST /api/upload (admin auth, multipart form 'file' field), GET /api/files/{path} (serves files). Product model has media_paths: List[str]. Frontend uses hidden file input with data-testid='media-file-input' and upload button data-testid='media-upload-btn'. MediaGallery component on Products page handles both media_paths (new) and image_url (legacy backward compatibility). Max 4 files per product enforced in frontend, allowed types: JPG, PNG, WEBP, MP4, MOV.
